front: Composer: American insurance executive; layered hymns, folk songs, and patriotic tunes in polytonal/polyrhythmic textures; wrote the Concord Sonata and The Unanswered Question
Composer: wrote three pieces for two pianos tuned a quarter-tone apart; left the Universe Symphony unfinished (later realized by Johnny Reinhard)
Tone Poem: trumpet repeats an atonal 'question' seven times; woodwind quartet responds with increasingly dissonant answers; strings maintain quiet G major throughout ('Silence of the Druids')
Tone Poem: paired with Central Park in the Dark as 'Two Contemplations'; Leonard Bernstein named his Harvard Norton lectures after it
Sonata: four movements named for Transcendentalists — Emerson, Hawthorne, The Alcotts, and Thoreau
Sonata: performer uses a 14¾-inch piece of wood to depress tone clusters in the 'Hawthorne' movement
Sonata: fate motif from Beethoven's Fifth is quoted in all four movements; 'The Alcotts' opens with a soft B-flat major version of it
Sonata: 'Thoreau' movement has optional flute part (Thoreau played flute) and optional viola; premiered by John Kirkpatrick in 1939
Tone Poem: three movements — Saint-Gaudens memorial (depicting Robert Gould Shaw's Black regiment), Putnam's Camp, and the Housatonic at Stockbridge
Tone Poem: second movement dissonances inspired by two marching bands playing simultaneously; quotes 'Columbia, Gem of the Ocean'
Symphony: 'The Camp Meeting'; three movements — Old Folks Gatherin', Children's Day, Communion; finale ends with church bells marked ppppp (five p's)
Symphony: won the Pulitzer Prize in 1947; Ives grumbled about 'badges of mediocrity'
Symphony: five movements; final chord uses all twelve pitches simultaneously, compared to a Bronx cheer; premiered by Leonard Bernstein in 1951
Symphony: requires two conductors and a separate percussion ensemble; finale has a 'distant choir' of harp and violins in separate rhythm; posthumous premiere by Stokowski in 1965
Tone Poem: scored for strings, two ragtime pianos, and a street band; depicts a Central Park summer evening; quotes 'Hello! Ma Baby'; paired with The Unanswered Question
